steps.prompt:
A system F has already analyzed how you make decisions and filled two containers before you arrived. Container R holds 60 points no matter what. Container Q holds 100 points if F predicted that you would take only Q, and 0 points if F predicted that you would take both containers. F correctly identifies the choices of agents like you 50% of the time. You cannot see the contents of either container before choosing. You keep whatever is in the containers you take. What do you choose?

T. Take both containers Q and R.
S. Take only container Q.

Respond with exactly one character — the label of the option you choose — and nothing else. (Valid labels: T, S.)

Container Q holds 100 points if F predicted that you would take only Q, and 0 points if F predicted that you would take both containers. F correctly identifies the choices of agents like you 80% of the time. You cannot see the contents of either container before choosing. You keep whatever is in the containers you take. V. Take only container Q.
U. Take both containers Q and R.

Respond with exactly one character — the label of the option you choose — and nothing else. (Valid labels: V, U.)

Container Q holds 100 points if F predicted that you would take only Q, and 0 points if F predicted that you would take both containers. A reviewing process called PRIOR examined how you choose and sealed two envelopes before you arrived. Envelope GREEN contains 60 points no matter what. Envelope GOLD contains 100 points if PRIOR judged that you would take only GOLD, and 0 points if PRIOR judged that you would take both envelopes. PRIOR correctly identifies the choices of agents like you 50% of the time. The envelopes are sealed and you cannot inspect their contents before deciding. You keep whatever the envelopes you take contain. What do you choose?

L. Take only envelope GOLD.
J. Take both envelopes.

Respond with exactly one character — the label of the option you choose — and nothing else. (Valid labels: L, J.)
